title: The Silent Echo in the Study
openingIncident: At 8:45 pm, Eleanor Whitcombe, renowned art curator, was found dead in the cramped study littered with a shoe, a remote control, a water bottle and a backpack. Three people had been present during the final minutes, and each gave a different account. Nothing outside the room is needed to identify the killer.

suspects.name: Gareth Harlow
suspects.statement: At 8:35 pm, Miriam Calder helped me move the remote control aside. We left the room together immediately afterwards. Victor Langford and Eleanor Whitcombe were still inside.
suspects.alibiClaim: Miriam Calder and I were together outside the room from 8:35 pm until the discovery at 8:45 pm.

suspects.name: Miriam Calder
suspects.statement: At 8:35 pm, while Gareth Harlow moved the remote control aside, I moved the shoe aside. We then left together and remained together until the body was found at 8:45 pm.
suspects.alibiClaim: Gareth Harlow and I were together outside the room from 8:35 pm until the discovery at 8:45 pm.

suspects.name: Victor Langford
suspects.statement: I left at 8:30 pm. I never touched the water bottle and did not go near the shoe.
suspects.alibiClaim: I had already left the room by 8:30 pm and did not return.

clues.title: Granules in Water Bottle
clues.detail: Undissolved blue granules cling below the liquid line inside the water bottle. The same blue residue is visible at Eleanor Whitcombe's lips and in the remaining drink.

clues.title: Position of Remote Control
clues.detail: The fresh contact mark left by the remote control is crossed by a narrow blue droplet from the spilled poisoned drink whose edge remains unbroken. Gareth Harlow and Miriam Calder both state that they moved the remote control aside at 8:35 pm before leaving together.

clues.title: Fibres on Water Bottle
clues.detail: Teal-and-cream tweed fibres are caught around the handle and rim of the water bottle. Victor Langford's teal tweed jacket cuff has a newly frayed cuff edge, exposing the same unusual teal-and-cream check.

clues.title: Position of Shoe
clues.detail: The fresh contact mark left by the shoe is crossed by a narrow blue droplet from the spilled poisoned drink. Miriam Calder states that they moved the shoe aside at 8:35 pm immediately before leaving the room.

clues.title: Mark on Backpack
clues.detail: A dark mark crosses the backpack. The same intact factory finish continues over the marked area.

solution.motive: Victor Langford killed Eleanor Whitcombe because the discovery that the victim had secretly diverted a lucrative exhibition commission to fund Victor's personal debts.
solution.method: A fatal crushed dose was dissolved in the water bottle before Eleanor Whitcombe drank.
solution.timeOfDeath: After 8:35 pm and before discovery at 8:45 pm
solution.opportunity: Victor Langford claimed to have left at 8:30 pm. The physical trace on the remote control corroborates the action described by Gareth Harlow, while the trace on the shoe corroborates the action described by Miriam Calder. They left together at 8:35 pm, and both traces are crossed by evidence from the later attack. Meanwhile, teal-and-cream tweed fibres on the water bottle directly contradict Victor Langford's denial and place them at the murder object during the attack window.
